title: Teaching
text: I teach risk management and insurance courses across the undergraduate, master’s, and Ph.D. programs at FSU. For current schedules, see the FSU Department of Risk Management/Insurance.

title: Teaching
text:
Florida State University

RMI 3011 — Risk Management/Insurance Undergraduate. Introduction to the principles of risk management and insurance and their application to personal and business pure risk problems. [Catalog]


RMI 4115 — Life/Health Insurance Undergraduate. Personal and business life and health insurance needs, plan characteristics, rating, underwriting, and financial analysis. [Catalog]


RMI 4292 — Property and Casualty Insurer Operations Undergraduate. The composition, financial structure, and operations of the property-casualty insurance industry, with special consideration of consumer problems and regulatory responses. [Catalog]


RMI 5710 — Insurance Company Operations Master’s (online). Fundamentals of risk, the management of pure risk, insurance mechanisms, insurer operations, and the evolution of enterprise risk management. [Catalog]


RMI 6395 — Seminar on the Theory of Risk and Insurance Ph.D. Review of the theoretical foundations of risk and insurance: the concept of risk, contributions from related disciplines, determinants of insurance consumption and risk management decisions, and industry dynamics. [Catalog]

title: Teaching
text:
University of Georgia

RMIN 4000 — Risk Management and Insurance Undergraduate (Summer 2014 – Spring 2017). Risk identification, risk evaluation, and risk treatment methods, with emphasis on insurance as a risk management tool. Designed for non-majors as well as a foundation for advanced RMI coursework.

title: Other
text:
Service & Editorial

Associate Editor, Risk Management and Insurance Review (2025–present).
Chair, ARIA Kulp-Wright Book Award Committee (2026); committee member (2020, 2023, 2025).
Secretary, Southern Risk and Insurance Association (2025–2026); board member (2019–2021, 2024–2025).
Member, ARIA Program Committee (2020, 2023–2026); ARIA Nomination Committee (2022); ARIA Hagen Family Foundation Travel Award Committee (2018, 2020).

Ad-hoc reviewer for Accounting Forum, Applied Economics, Geneva Papers on Risk and Insurance—Issues and Practice, Journal of Contingencies and Crisis Management, Journal of Financial Research, Journal of Insurance Issues, Journal of Insurance Regulation, Journal of Risk and Insurance, North American Actuarial Journal, Risk Management and Insurance Review, Risks, and The Financial Review.

title: Evan M. Eastman
text:
I am the Independent Life and Accident Insurance Associate Professor and Research Coordinator for the Risk Management and Insurance Center at Florida State University in the Herbert Wertheim College of Business. I serve as a faculty member in the Department of Risk Management/Insurance, Real Estate, and Legal Studies in the Dr. William T. Hold/The Alliance’s Program in Risk Management and Insurance.
My research focuses on empirical archival insurance economics. Active areas include financial accounting, taxation, corporate risk management, and real estate.

title: Evan M. Eastman
text:
Contact
Dr. William T. Hold/The Alliance’s Program in Risk Management & Insurance 402 W. Gaines Street  Wertheim Center for Business Excellence 4814 Herbert Wertheim College for Business Florida State University Tallahassee, FL, 32301 eeastman@wertheim.fsu.edu

title: Research
text: My research focuses on empirical insurance economics, with emphasis on financial accounting, taxation, regulation, and enterprise risk management. For a full record, see my CV.
